data.title: Sensex, Nifty marginally up ahead of US inflation data; broader markets outperform
data.description:
The BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices continued to outperform, rising 0.3 percent and 0.4 percent, respectively.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The Sensex and Nifty edged into the green by midday on December 11 ahead of key inflation data from the U.S. and India's CPI data on December 12. The indices were lifted by gains in IT and FMCG stocks.

At 11.50 AM, the Sensex was up 161 points or 0.2 percent at 81,671, and the Nifty was up 61 points or 0.3 percent at 24,671. About 1,996 shares advanced, 1,379 shares declined, and 90 shares remained unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The biggest positive for the market is the return of the FIIs and the consequent strength in the largecaps, particularly in banking and IT," said V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services. In December so far, FIIs have made net purchases of nearly Rs 14,000 crore, after their selling spree in October and November.

When it comes to sectoral performance, Nifty Auto rose 0.5 percent, bolstered by gains in Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M), Maruti Suzuki, and Bajaj Auto after Jefferies named M&M a top pick in the sector.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

The Nifty Metal index rose nearly a percent led by gains in Vedanta, Hindalco, and Tata Steel on expectations of monetary policy easing by top consumer China. A few days back, Beijing announced plans to adopt "moderately loose monetary policies" next year and introduce steps to boost consumption, marking its first such policy shift in 14 years.

Cement stocks were also in focus amid price hikes by dealers, marking the first increase after 4–5 months of stagnant margins. The hikes are attributed to rising demand from the real estate sector, improved post-festive labor availability, and higher infrastructure orders. Shares of ACC, Dalmia Bharat, JK Cement, and JK Lakshmi Cement rose between 1–3 percent.

In individual stocks, BEML shares surged nearly 4 percent after securing a Rs 136 crore contract to supply high-mobility vehicles. AurionPro saw a 6 percent jump following a significant order win in Saudi Arabia for its iCashpro+ platform.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices continued to outperform, rising 0.3 percent and 0.4 percent, respectively.

Also Read | MobiKwik IPO fully subscribed within first hour on Day 1; retail portion booked 5x

"Mid-sized companies benefiting from export demand in Europe and the U.S., previously catered to by China, are driving growth. Mid-size firms aligned with manufacturing are likely to sustain their outperformance," said Sandip Agarwal, Fund Manager and Co-founder of Sowilo Investment Managers LLP.

However, few market experts cautioned against elevated valuations in this segment.

Sameet Chavan, Head of Technical and Derivative Research at Angel One, observed that the 24,500–24,400 zone has provided strong support for Nifty, keeping bulls in control. He expects Nifty 50 to face resistance at around 24750–24800. "A sustained move beyond this hurdle could pave the way for 25000 and higher levels," he said.

Among the Nifty 50, UltraTech Cement, Britannia, Nestle, Grasim, and Shriram Finance emerged as the top gainers, rising 1–2 percent. On the flip side, Adani Ports, HCLTech, Adani Enterprises, ICICI Bank, and Wipro declined 0.3–0.8 percent.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: ITI shares dive 7% as profit booking seeps in from record high
data.description:
ITI stock has been witnessing strong trading volumes in the last three sessions.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of ITI witnessed strong selling pressure and plunged 7 percent on December 11 as investors rushed to book profits off the stock after its stellar upmove in the last three sessions. Driven by strong trading volumes, the stock clocked in gains of around 34 percent in the past three sessions that took it to a record high of Rs 404 on Tuesday, leaving ample room for investors to take cash out partial profits.

Accordingly, at 11.26 am, shares of ITI were trading at Rs 363.80 on the NSE. Aside from the stellar surge in the stock in the last three trading sessions, it has also raked in gains of over 24 percent in the past three months.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company also announced a slew of positive developments in the past month which further bolstered investor sentiment around the stock.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Firstly, in early-November, the company emerged as the lowest bidder for three packages of the BharatNet Phase-3 project, valued at Rs 4,559 crore. BharatNet Phase-3, comprising 16 packages across all states and union territories, involves the design, supply, construction, installation, upgradation, operation, and maintenance of the middle-mile network under a Design Build Operate and Maintain (DBOM) model, as tendered by BSNL.

Later in the same month, the company secured another Rs 95 crore contract from the Directorate of Geology & Mining, Government of Uttarakhand, for the Mining Digital Transformation & Surveillance System (MDTSS) project. Under this contract, the company will develop, implement, and maintain MDTSS across Dehradun, Haridwar, Udham Singh Nagar, and Nainital, covering 40 check gates.

data.title: Sensex, Nifty flat ahead of US inflation data; broader markets outperform
data.description:
The BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices continued to outperform, rising 0.3 percent and 0.4 percent, respectively.

The Sensex and Nifty remained largely steady on December 11, reflecting cautious investor sentiment ahead of crucial inflation data from the U.S. later today and India's CPI print on December 12. Both indices tilted slightly towards the green, buoyed by gains in IT and auto stocks.

At 11:30 AM, the Sensex was up 123 points or 0.2 percent at 81,633, and the Nifty was up 46 points or 0.2 percent at 24,656. About 1,970 shares advanced, 1,385 shares declined, and 93 shares remained unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The biggest positive for the market is the return of the FIIs and the consequent strength in the largecaps, particularly in banking and IT," said V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services. In December so far, FIIs have made net purchases of nearly Rs 14,000 crore, after their selling spree in October and November.

data.title: MobiKwik IPO fully subscribed within first hour on Day 1; retail portion booked 5x
data.description:
MobiKwik IPO fully subscribed within an hour of opening on Day 1.

One Mobikwik Systems' Rs 572-crore IPO was fully subscribed within the first hour of the issue opening on December 11, as investors bet on strong growth prospects in India's booming online payments market.

The fintech firm is taking bids from December 11-13 in a price range of Rs 265-279 per share, targeting a valuation of Rs 2,168 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The stock is likely to start trading on December 18.

The company, which counts non-bank lender Bajaj Finance, Peak XV and Abu Dhabi Investment Authority as existing shareholders, is selling only new shares in the IPO.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Why real estate majors are flocking to Mumbai’s redevelopment market
data.description:
Real Estate

Highlights

  • Mumbai has seen record registration of property transactions

  • With limited free land, there is a limitation to growth

  • Redevelopment is a win-win for all stakeholders

  • With land being scarce, redevelopment is soon becoming a project of choice for developers in Mumbai

 

The real estate market in Mumbai has achieved its best November on record, with 10,200 property transactions registered. However, this figure is lower than October's record of 12,960 sales, primarily driven by pent-up demand following the Shraadh period. For 2024,

data.title: Asian Granito stock zooms 11% as firm sets up JV with Shudh Investments and UK's Klyn Stone
data.description:
On a year-to-date basis, Asian Granito stock remained largely flat.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Asian Granito were buzzing in trade and surged over 11 percent on December 11 after the company signed a joint venture agreement with UK-based companies--Shudh Investments Ltd and Klyn Stone.

The three companies will together set-up a joint venture entity in England and Wales, named Klyn AGL Ltd, which will be involved in the trading business of large porcelain slabs, all type of tiles and quartz products throughout the UK. Under the agreement, each party has the right to appoint two directors to the board and subscribe to fresh capital issuances proportional to their shareholding.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Furthermore, all stakeholders will have the authority to restrict any changes to the capital structure of Klyn AGL. As part of the arrangement, 2.5 lakh ordinary shares, valued at one euro each, will be issued to the stakeholders.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

At 10.29 am, shares of Asian Granito were trading at Rs 75.24 on the NSE.

With the sharp jump in the today's session, trading volumes in the counter also skyrocketed. As many as 40 lakh shares changed hands so far, already surpassing the one-month daily traded average of six lakh shares by a wide margin.

Aside from today's sharp surge in the counter, the stock has witnessed a rather weak trend in the year so far. On a year-to-date basis, the stock remained largely flat, however, it has slipped over 12 percent in the past three months, primarily due to its weak Q2 numbers.

data.title: Mishtann Foods stock snaps 3-day losing streak as 2.3% equity exchanged in a block deal
data.description:
According to Sebi's notice, the company inflated its sales and purchases by recording "fictitious transactions".

Snapping a three-day losing streak, shares of Mishtann Foods gained almost four percent on the bourses on December 11 after a large deal on the bourses.

Around 2.3 percent equity or 2.4 crore worth Rs 19.4 crore exchanged hands in a block deal window on December 11.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 10.15 am, shares of Mishtann Foods were quoting Rs 9.28 per share, higher by 3.7 percent compared to the previous close.

Follow our market blog to catch all the updates

Over the previous three sessions, Mishtann Foods shares tanked over 40 percent. Investors dumped the stock after the company received a show cause notice from the market regulator, Sebi.

The show cause notice was issued over the company's Rs 100 crore fund which Sebi stated was misappropriated or diverted through group entities. SEBI's whole-time member, Ashwani Bhatia, had highlighted "misrepresentation of large proportions" in Mishtann Foods' financial statements.

According to Sebi's notice, the company inflated its sales and purchases by recording "fictitious transactions" involving "fake or non-existent entities." These entities were allegedly created using the names of Mishtann Foods' promoters and their relatives.

However, the company refuted Sebi's allegations while stating that its legal team is "making efforts to address the queries and take appropriate actions."

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: PNC Infra logs biggest gain in six months, skyrockets 12% on bagging early completion bonus
data.description:
PNC Infra also boasts a strong order book which stood at Rs 19,909 crore at the end of the September quarter.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of PNC Infratech zoomed nearly 12 percent in early trade on December 11, clocking its biggest single-day gains in six months. This comes after the company earned a bonus of Rs 4.4 crore due to the early completion of a road project in Uttar Pradesh.

The company completed a Rs 738 crore project, awarded by the National Highways Authority of India, for the four laning of national highway-53, two months before its stipulated time, making it eligible to earn an early completion bonus.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 10.02 am, shares of PNC Infratech were trading at Rs 340.75 on the NSE. With the steep upmove in the today's session, trading volumes in the counter also skyrocketed. As many as 69 lakh shares changed hands so far, already surpassing the one-month daily traded average of 10 lakh shares by a wide margin.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

PNC Infra also boasts a strong order book which stood at Rs 19,909 crore at the end of the September quarter. The order book by the end of the first half of FY25 has already surpassed that seen in entire FY24. The company's order book at the end of FY24 stood at Rs15,400 crore, with the majority comprising of road EPC (Engineering, Construction and Procurement) projects, making up for around 76 percent of the total.

However, sentiment for the stock has taken a hit in the past two months after the Ministry of Roads (MoRTH) disqualified the construction firm and its two subsidiaries from participating in any new tender process of the ministry for a year due to a bribery case filed by the CBI involving four employees of the company.

On that account, brokerage firm Sharekhan had revised its estimates lower for the stock to and also reduced its valuation multiple to factor in the medium-term uncertainties with respect to its disqualification by MoRTH. Sharekhan retained its 'buy' call on the stock with a price target of Rs 400.

data.title: Nisus Finance Services shares list at 25% premium over IPO price on BSE SME
data.description:
The net proceeds will be used to strengthen fund management infrastructure

Shares of Nisus Finance Services made a decent start on its stock market debut on December 11 after listing at Rs 225, commanding a premium of 25 percent over the issue price of Rs 180 per share on the BSE SME platform.

The listing, however, missed grey market estimates where shares were trading with a premium of over 52 percent. The grey market is an unofficial ecosystem where shares start trading much before the offer opens for subscription and continue to trade till the listing day.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

The Rs 114.24 crore public offer, a mix of a fresh issue and an offer for sale, received robust subscription figures as the issue was subscribed 192 times. Non-institutional investors were at the forefront, buying a staggering 451 times the portion reserved for them. Retail investors purchased nearly 140 times while QIBs purchased 94 times the portion reserved for them.

Founded in 2013, the company provides financial solutions including transaction advisory, fund and asset management, private equity, venture debt, and capital solutions. Its advisory services support transactions like sales, joint ventures, and capital structuring, connecting businesses with private equity and financial institutions. The fund and asset management segment focuses on growth capital, project financing, and managing alternative investment funds in real estate and infrastructure.

The net proceeds will be used to strengthen fund management infrastructure at IFSC Gift City, DIFC-Dubai, and FSC-Mauritius, cover fundraising and distribution costs, invest in capital augmentation for Nisus Fincorp Private Limited, and support general corporate purposes.

data.title: Ambit retains Zomato as its core holding in Dec, says business model only partly imitable by Swiggy, Zepto
data.description:
Zomato's 58 percent market share in food delivery is expected to grow beyond 60 percent in the next few years and, more importantly, Zomato is likely to capture over 75 percent of the industry’s profits, giving it an edge over Swiggy.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Replicating Zomato’s cost-advantage model is challenging for competitors, especially given its market share and higher advertisement revenues, said Ambit Asset Managers in its monthly newsletter to its shareholders while retaining Zomato as its core holding in its portfolios in December.

It stated that while many competitors resort to cash-burning strategies and heavy discounting, these approaches are unsustainable, and Zomato’s model, with its cost advantages and market leadership, makes imitation difficult for other players.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The reason? Zomato has a high average order value in food delivery as well as quick commerce. Competitors who try to copy the business model of Zomato can only partially imitate this, says the asset manager as increasing average order value requires innovation and superior customer insights.

Also, competitors chasing unprofitable market segments just to boost average order value can derail its business model if they go behind unprofitable segments like bulk groceries or low-margin electronics.

The asset manager says that Zomato is a leader in advertising because of its 58 percent market share in food delivery, which gives it an edge over competitors and this advantage is likely to last. It further adds that a player with more than 50 percent market share will invariably have a relatively much higher share of industry's advertisement income.

While all players are now trying to increase their advertisement income, it will remain a sustainable competitive advantage for the leader which is Zomato, said the asset manager.
Interestingly, the newsletter further states that Zomato's 58 percent market share in food delivery is expected to grow beyond 60 percent in the next few years and, more importantly, Zomato is likely to capture over 75 percent of the industry’s profits, giving it an edge over Swiggy.

The competition arena with Zepto, Dmart Ready and BigBasket

Zepto, with about 28 percent market share in quick commerce, has been very aggressive with discounts to attract customers. However, it faces disadvantages compared to Zomato, which has 45 percent market share in quick commerce, and Swiggy, which has a 25 percent market share. The disadvantages include higher customer acquisition costs, lack of synergy, and smaller scale.
In the case of Dmart Ready, the management plans to stick to delivering large basket, slotted deliveries and does not intend to compete in the quick commerce convenience segment.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In contrast, BigBasket (with its three formats: BigBasket, BBNow, and Neuflash) is reportedly aiming to compete in the quick commerce convenience space by reducing delivery times. However, monthly bulk purchases are unlikely to succeed in this model due to high costs and operational challenges, they stated in the newsletter.

Amazon and Walmart are stepping into the quick commerce space because their traditional e-commerce business models are facing disruption. Both companies already operate loss-making businesses in e-commerce, and expanding into quick commerce - another challenging segment—will likely deepen their financial losses, the newsletter says.

Additionally, as foreign-owned companies, they are more exposed to regulatory risks, since retail rules in India are stricter for foreign players compared to domestic ones, added the newsletter.
